preview_text:
The model favors Australia at 53.5% to secure victory, placing Egypt's chances at 17.4%. This represents a substantial gap, with the underdog offered little more than a one-in-six probability under the statistical forecast.

The expected match shape centers on a draw at 29.1%, suggesting meaningful competitive balance despite Australia's modeled edge. The win probabilities are separated by 36 percentage points, indicating the model sees a clear favorite but not an overwhelming one. A draw remains genuinely plausible as the second-most-likely outcome, meaning the contest could remain undecided through regulation.

Model and sportsbook consensus diverge markedly. The market rates Australia's win probability at roughly 29%, some 24 percentage points below the model, while pricing Egypt's chances at 38% — more than double the model's assessment. Polymarket aligns closely with the sportsbook view. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the divergence is substantial enough that bettors should note the disagreement carefully.
